summary: The Chief Nursing Officer role is an unsuitable match as its 24/7 on-call demands and 60-hour work weeks directly conflict with your priorities for personal wellbeing and predictable hours. Furthermore, the position requires high-level quantitative reasoning for multi-million-dollar budgets and strategic judgment over protocols, which misaligns with your strengths in procedural work and your preference to avoid complex math and analytical deduction. Given the extreme stress and high risk of burnout, this executive career path is unsustainable for your stated goals.
